title: DisciplineModel
description: Working-tool description of an academic discipline for Kairoskopion's disciplinary landscape registry. NOT an encyclopedia entry — fields are chosen so downstream agents (semantic_profiler, fit_assessor, venue_candidate_screening) can decide which objects, methods, questions, evidence, and genres this discipline legitimately admits.

schema_version.description: Schema version that produced this record. Bump on additive change; document under data/disciplinary_landscape/schema/CHANGELOG.md.

model_version.description: Semver of this particular discipline card. Bumps when content (not schema) is revised.

discipline_id.description: Stable id, kebab-case, namespaced by region/source: ru-vak-5.7.8, eu-erc-sh4-philosophy, intl-oecd-ford-6.3, etc.

display_names.description: Localized names. At least one of ru / en must be present.

region.description: Where this discipline lives institutionally. 'international' is for disciplines that have no single national home.

aliases.description: Alternative names, mis-spellings, near-synonyms, historical names. Used by the matcher for fuzzy candidate selection.

source_status.description: Lifecycle status. New cards are llm_draft. Cards proposed by semantic_profiler on a real case are 'candidate'. Curator promotes to user_confirmed. See operating-loop notes.

curator_notes.description: Free-form notes by the human curator. Null until reviewed.

source_id.description: Stable id within the source (e.g. ВАК '5.7.8', ERC 'SH4.11', OECD FORD '6.3.1').

excerpt.description: Short verbatim excerpt the LLM read from this source.

confidence_by_section.description: Per-field confidence in {paradigm, objects, methods, key_authors, history, ...}. Values: high / medium / low / null.

unknowns.description: Fields the seeder/curator could not determine. Surfaces to UI.

disputed_fields.description: Fields where sources disagree. Surfaces to UI as a 'needs review' badge.

paradigm.description: One paragraph: how this discipline frames its world. NOT a textbook definition — what makes it recognizable as itself.

epistemic_regime.description: How this discipline KNOWS. What counts as a valid epistemic operation here? (e.g. 'phenomenological description of lived experience as primary evidence'; 'statistical inference from controlled experiments'; 'genealogical reconstruction of concepts').

forms_of_evidence.description: What counts as proof in this discipline. Examples: 'first-person phenomenological reports', 'historical archive', 'controlled-experiment p-values', 'conceptual analysis showing logical entailment', 'ethnographic field notes', 'corpus-based frequency data'.

canonical_questions.description: Questions this discipline knows how to FORMULATE (regardless of whether it answers them). A discipline is defined by the questions it accepts as legitimate.

typical_problem_forms.description: Shapes a research problem takes here: 'tension between X and Y', 'apparent paradox of Z', 'inadequacy of current concept of W', 'mismatch between theory and practice in V'.

legitimate_objects.description: Objects this discipline legitimately studies. Examples for philosophy of technology: 'technical artifact', 'mediation of action', 'sociotechnical assemblage', 'technical mentality'.

illegitimate_or_borderline_objects.description: Objects this discipline does NOT legitimately study OR borderline cases that mark its edges. Critical for fit assessment — knowing what does NOT fit is as important as what does.

argument_styles.description: Modes of argumentation accepted as proper here: 'conceptual analysis', 'transcendental argument', 'genealogical critique', 'comparative case study', 'thought experiment', 'phenomenological description', 'formal proof', 'statistical inference'.

publication_genres.description: Genres this discipline actually publishes in: 'theoretical essay', 'empirical study with IMRAD', 'philosophical commentary', 'systematic review', 'book chapter', 'critical edition', 'review essay'.

institutional_forms.description: Where this discipline lives institutionally: 'philosophy departments', 'STS programs', 'педагогические институты', 'institutes of psychology', 'iSchools', 'AHRC-funded research centres'.

russian_specificity.description: Russian-tradition specifics — divergences from international namesakes, native authors who define the field, unique objects/methods. Null when irrelevant (international-only discipline).

international_mapping.description: discipline_ids of equivalent / overlapping disciplines in other regions. Critical for cross-region search and venue fit.

methods.description: Concrete research methods practiced here. Subset of forms_of_evidence operationalized as procedures.

instruments.description: Specific intellectual instruments: 'concept analysis', 'reflective equilibrium', 'phenomenological reduction', 'discourse analysis software', 'fMRI', 'corpus tools'.

ontologies.description: Core concepts the discipline operates with as primitive entities: 'Dasein', 'Lebenswelt', 'mediation', 'assemblage', 'habitus', 'paradigm'.

discipline_relevance.description: One line: why this author matters here specifically.

history.description: When the discipline emerged, what it split from, what split from it. Not a Wikipedia summary — the structural shifts.

boundaries.description: What this discipline is OFTEN confused with and the criterion that distinguishes them. Examples: 'Not to be confused with cultural anthropology (empirical) — philosophical anthropology is a priori reflection on the human as such'.

adjacent.description: discipline_ids of neighbouring disciplines (in this or other regions). Different from international_mapping — these are NEIGHBOURS, not equivalents.

typical_venues.description: venue_ids from Kairoskopion's venue registry, when known. Empty until venue registry has matching entries.

first_seen_in_case.description: case_id where this discipline first surfaced (for 'candidate' records proposed by semantic_profiler).

times_seen.description: How many cases have invoked this discipline. Drives ordering/priority for the matcher.
